Name: Llama 3.3 / 4
OneLiner: The most widely self-hosted open-weight family — broad tool support, from 8B to 400B+ MoE variants.

Name: Qwen 2.5 / 3
OneLiner: Strong coding and multilingual performance across a very wide size range, including small edge-friendly models.

Name: DeepSeek-V3 / R1
OneLiner: Frontier-level reasoning (R1) and general chat (V3) at a fraction of the usual training/serving cost. The default pull here is the 7B distilled R1 model, not the full 671B.

Name: Mistral / Mixtral
OneLiner: Efficient dense and mixture-of-experts models, popular for self-hosting on modest hardware.

Name: Gemma 2 / 3
OneLiner: Small, well-behaved models tuned to run comfortably on a single consumer GPU.

Name: Phi-4
OneLiner: Small model trained on heavily-curated synthetic data — punches well above its parameter count.

Name: Command R+
OneLiner: Built specifically for RAG and tool use, with strong citation/grounding behavior.

Name: Zephyr
OneLiner: A Mistral fine-tune tuned specifically to be helpful and conversational — a favorite in the self-hosted chatbot community.

Name: Llama 3.2
OneLiner: The small end of the Llama family — fast, low-RAM, and still genuinely useful for everyday chat.

Name: TinyLlama
OneLiner: A 1.1B model that runs on almost anything — good for testing the plumbing before committing to a bigger download.

Name: StarCoder2
OneLiner: Code-specialized model trained on The Stack — a solid fully-open (data included) coding option.

Name: Qwen 2.5 Coder
OneLiner: A coding-specialized fine-tune of Qwen 2.5 — strong at code completion, review, and multi-language tasks.

Name: CodeGemma
OneLiner: Google's code-focused Gemma variant — code completion and generation, a different lineage from the Qwen/BigCode options.

Name: LLaVA
OneLiner: Vision-language model — describe images, answer questions about screenshots/photos, right from your own PC.

Name: Moondream
OneLiner: A tiny (~1.9B) vision-language model — much lighter than LLaVA, still useful for basic image Q&A.

Name: Nomic Embed Text
OneLiner: Not a chat model — an embedding model for semantic search/RAG pipelines. Pairs with any chat model above.

Name: Aya
OneLiner: Built specifically for multilingual coverage (100+ languages) — a good pick if you're not working primarily in English.
